AuditIQ follows a simple intelligence pipeline:
The system is designed to answer one core question:
Where are the exceptions, what is the risk, and what action should be taken?
AuditIQ identifies each document type automatically.
AuditIQ compares values across all documents.
Invoice amount exceeds PO amount by 18%.
Review invoice approval and verify pricing changes with procurement team.
Results appear inside the AuditIQ dashboard.

Every exception generated by AuditIQ follows a standard structure.
Invoice unit price exceeds purchase order price.
Verify contract pricing before payment approval.
This structure ensures consistency across all exception categories.
Potential fraud, duplicate payment, or major financial exposure.
The analyst identifies issues in minutes instead of manually reviewing documents.
This validates the core AuditIQ value proposition.

The goal is to reduce the time required to move from:
AuditIQ evolves from a detection engine into a decision-support platform.
Why is it wrong, how important is it, and what should happen next?

Users can interact with exceptions using natural language.

The objective is to move beyond detection and provide context.
For every exception, AuditIQ generates recommended actions.
Verify contract pricing and invoice approval history.
Request goods receipt confirmation before payment processing.
Hold payment and perform duplicate validation review.
Verify received quantity against purchase order records.
Not all exceptions carry equal business impact.
The system ranks exceptions from highest to lowest priority.

These metrics help users focus attention on the most important risks.

Users spend less time identifying issues and more time resolving them.
Blueprint V2 extends AuditIQ beyond exception detection by introducing investigation
intelligence, root cause analysis, prioritization, and AI-generated recommendations.
The platform evolves from identifying problems to helping users understand and resolve them.
The purpose of this section is to define the technical architecture of the AuditIQ MVP.
This architecture translates the product vision into a buildable system.
The objective is not to build a complete enterprise platform.
The objective is to demonstrate the core AuditIQ value proposition:
AI-powered exception intelligence for procurement document matching.
Each component has a clearly defined responsibility.
The architecture centers around exception intelligence generation.
AuditIQ MVP consists of six major components.
Better support for AI and document-processing workflows.
Convert uploaded files into structured business data.
The technical architecture is designed to support this evolution without major redesign.
This section establishes the complete technical foundation of AuditIQ.

The purpose of this section is to define how Artificial Intelligence powers AuditIQ.
While the Technical Architecture defines how the system operates and the Data Architecture
defines how information is stored, the AI Architecture defines how AuditIQ generates
The objective is not simply to process documents.
The objective is to transform raw procurement records into actionable exception intelligence.
The platform does not rely on a single AI model.
Instead, multiple AI components work together.
Each stage contributes to the final outcome.
The AI layer must answer four questions:
What should the user do next?
These questions define the MVP intelligence workflow.
The architecture follows a sequential intelligence pipeline.
Enterprise-grade OCR models with higher accuracy.
Convert unstructured text into structured business records.
Preserve business meaning while creating structured records.
Identify discrepancies that matter to business operations.
Move beyond raw comparisons and identify actionable issues.
Hold payment approval and perform duplicate validation review.
Ensure every AI decision is understandable.
For every exception, AuditIQ should provide:
Every AI output should include confidence levels.
It does not make financial decisions autonomously.
Audit and financial controls require accountability.
Risk scores generated from historical behavior.
Continuous risk assessment across integrated systems.

The purpose of this section is to define how AuditIQ will be deployed, hosted, secured,
The Deployment Architecture translates the technical system into a running product.
The primary objective is to ensure that the AuditIQ MVP can be reliably demonstrated during the
hackathon while maintaining a clear path toward enterprise deployment.
The MVP prioritizes simplicity while preserving future expansion opportunities.
This architecture minimizes operational complexity while remaining production-capable.
Separate file storage from transactional data.
Processing should remain independent from the frontend.
Security is critical because procurement documents may contain sensitive business information.
Authentication is excluded from the MVP.
All critical activities should be logged.
Prevent loss of uploaded records and analysis results.

These requirements exceed MVP scope but guide future planning.
